name: Night Hag
immunities: charm effects, cold, disease, fear effects, fire, sleep;
melee: claw +18 (3d4+13 S) or incapacitator +18 (3d4+13 nonlethal B; critical staggered [DC 18])
ranged: aphelion laser pistol +16 (3d4+9 F; critical burn 1d4)

spell_like_abilities.spells:
1/day—
bestow curse
(DC 20),
deep slumber
(DC 20)
3/day—
inflict pain
(DC 19),
invisibility
At will—
command
(DC 18),
magic missile
At will (with
heartstone
)—
ethereal jaunt
Constant—
detect magic
,
detect tech

skills: Bluff +22, Diplomacy +17, Disguise +17, Intimidate +17, Mysticism +22, Sense Motive +17
gear: gold AbadarCorp travel suit, aphelion laser pistol with 1 high-capacity battery (40 charges), incapacitator with 1 battery (20 charges),
organization: solitary or coven (3 hags of any type)

special_abilities.name: Dream Haunting (Su)
special_abilities.description:
A night hag can visit the dreams of chaotic or evil targets by using her
heartstone
to become ethereal, then hovering over the creature. Once she does so, she rides on the victim’s back until dawn. The sleeper suffers tormenting dreams and takes 1 point of Constitution drain upon awakening.

special_abilities.name: Heartstone (Su)
special_abilities.description:
Every night hag owns a
heartstone
—a special gemstone worth at least 4,000 credits that she wears as a periapt. The
heartstone
makes her immune to diseases and grants her special powers, such as the ability to cast
ethereal jaunt
at will as a spell-like ability. Once per day, a night hag can trap the soul of a recently dead creature within the
heartstone
(Will DC 23 negates); the creature can’t have been dead more than 8 rounds, and once its soul is trapped, it can’t be returned to life by any means (even
miracle
or
wish
). The soul can be freed only by destroying the
heartstone
or rendering it nonmagical, and a
heartstone
can hold only one soul at a time. A
heartstone
’s magic is fueled by the night hag’s spirit and proximity—once separated from its owner (or upon the night hag’s death), a
heartstone
functions as a 9th-level worn magic item for only 24 hours before becoming a nonmagical gem again. A
heartstone
instantly cures any disease contracted by the wearer and provides a +2 enhancement bonus to all saving throws. A night hag that loses her
heartstone
is no longer immune to disease and can no longer use ethereal jaunt until it is replaced.

description:
Night hags trade in souls and revel in the grief they cause. They prey on mortals who are in their most vulnerable state, first toying with victims by haunting their dreams night after night. When the unfortunate mortal passes, the night hag traps their soul within her
heartstone
, eventually transferring the soul into another magical object that she can sell in the infernal markets of the outer planes.
Night hags vary in appearance, as they can resemble cruel, horned versions of almost any humanoid species. They can be anywhere between 3 and 8 feet tall and weigh between 100 and 400 pounds.

Dream Haunting (Su)
A night hag can visit the dreams of chaotic or evil targets by using her

to become ethereal, then hovering over the creature. Once she does so, she rides on the victim’s back until dawn. The sleeper suffers tormenting dreams and takes 1 point of Constitution drain upon awakening.
Heartstone (Su)
Every night hag owns a
heartstone
—a special gemstone worth at least 4,000 credits that she wears as a periapt. The
heartstone
makes her immune to diseases and grants her special powers, such as the ability to cast
ethereal jaunt
at will as a spell-like ability. Once per day, a night hag can trap the soul of a recently dead creature within the
heartstone
(Will DC 23 negates); the creature can’t have been dead more than 8 rounds, and once its soul is trapped, it can’t be returned to life by any means (even

’s magic is fueled by the night hag’s spirit and proximity—once separated from its owner (or upon the night hag’s death), a

functions as a 9th-level worn magic item for only 24 hours before becoming a nonmagical gem again. A
heartstone
instantly cures any disease contracted by the wearer and provides a +2 enhancement bonus to all saving throws. A night hag that loses her
heartstone

Description
Night hags trade in souls and revel in the grief they cause. They prey on mortals who are in their most vulnerable state, first toying with victims by haunting their dreams night after night. When the unfortunate mortal passes, the night hag traps their soul within her
heartstone
, eventually transferring the soul into another magical object that she can sell in the infernal markets of the outer planes.
Night hags vary in appearance, as they can resemble cruel, horned versions of almost any humanoid species. They can be anywhere between 3 and 8 feet tall and weigh between 100 and 400 pounds.
